MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-17-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant Chaparral Country Corporation'S Motion For Summary Judgment And Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Rya Nelson, Esq. 415-835-6777 Bracamontes & Vlasak for Plaintiff.
Melanie Smith, Esq. 619-564-3606 Hawkins Parnell for Defendant Chaparral County Corp. (In-person)
Court reporter: Esther Au, CSR #14594, 415-6991341, estheraucsr14594@gmail.com, Reported.

ARGUED. Defendant contests the tentative ruling.Ms. Smith argues. Ms. Nelson responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentaive ruling.

PLAINTIFF CHRISTINE KENDRICK's Motion To Strike Defendant Chaparral Country Corporation'S Motion For Summary Judgment And Summary Adjudication.

Plaintiff Christine Kendrick's Motion to Strike Defendant Chaparral Country Corporation's Motion for Summary Judgment and Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. The court construes the motion as a common law motion to strike. It is undisputed Defendant Chaparral Country Corporation did not file its motion at least 75 days before the date for which it is noticed. The motion, thus, was not timely filed. (See Cuff v. Grossmont Union High Sch. Dist. (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 582, 596 [motion for summary judgment must be filed at least 75 days before the time appointed for hearing].) Further, the motion was not timely served. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 437(a)(2) [notice of the motion and supporting papers must be served 75 days before the hearing]; McMahon v. Superior Court (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 112, 118 ["in light of the express statutory language, trial courts do not have authority to shorten the minimum notice period for summary judgment hearings"].) There is no evidence Defendant has yet served the motion it intends to have heard on January 22, 2025, and service at this time would be untimely. The court finds irrelevant Defendant's service of a prior, un-file motion and one or more errata. The parties and the court must be able to work from identical papers, which is not possible here. Even assuming the court could deem service of the un-filed motion and errata amounted to service of the complaint, that service was not complete 75 days before the noticed hearing. The court finds Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5 inapt. This is not a motion to strike a pleading as that term is defined in section 435.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Esther Au, CSR #14594, 415-6991341, estheraucsr14594@gmail.com, Reported. (301/JMQ)
